---
title: "Philip Felix"
subtitle: "Senior Data Scientist"
---

I help teams figure out what to measure and whether it's working — translating business questions into analytical plans, building self-serve dashboards, and moving organizations from "what happened" reporting to understanding what actually caused it. Most recently at Atlassian, where I spent 6 years in GTM data science building the company's first-ever ICP and Buying Group frameworks and owning the source-of-truth dashboards leadership relied on to make decisions. Before that, I was the first analyst hired into CS at Square, where I created the First Contact Resolution metric and built the tools the support org ran on.

I'm deeply curious about how AI is changing the way data teams work. At Atlassian I led that effort for a 30-person org — not by mandating tools, but by sitting down with people one-on-one until they were running LLM-assisted analyses on their own.

Before Atlassian and Square, I was doing ops at ZocDoc. Psychology degree, math post-bacc, and a teaching stint at General Assembly that permanently ruined my ability to write jargon without flinching.

## Featured Projects

### [Lead Scoring & Buying Group Identification](projects/lead-scoring.qmd)

You have 1,500 accounts and can only engage 200 this quarter. Which 200? This project builds a propensity model to answer that, plus a buying group analyzer that figures out *why* good accounts aren't closing and who's missing from the deal.

Python, Pandas, scikit-learn, Streamlit

### [Job Title Normalizer](projects/title-normalizer.qmd)

CRM job titles are a mess. "Sr. Dir. Mktg (EMEA)" and "Senior Director of Marketing" are the same person. This multi-task PyTorch classifier standardizes them into seniority levels and job functions, handling 15 categories of real-world CRM noise that break rule-based approaches.

Python, PyTorch, scikit-learn

### [AI Adoption Playbook](projects/ai-adoption-playbook.qmd)

How I took a 30-person data org from "what's an MCP server?" to running LLM-assisted analyses in weeks. Not theory. The actual steps, in order, with the blockers I hit and what I'd do differently.

Claude, MCP, Cursor, Databricks CLI

### [Adaptive Analyst Agent](projects/context-agent.qmd)

An LLM-powered analyst that answers the same business question differently depending on who's asking. A VP gets a board-level summary. A campaign manager gets channel attribution and budget recommendations. Same data, different context.

Python, Claude API, DuckDB, Streamlit

### [Marketing Measurement Under Hypergrowth](projects/marketing-measurement.qmd)

When a company grows from $1B to $30B in 15 months on mostly organic adoption, every standard measurement approach breaks. This interactive app simulates those dynamics — showing why attribution overcredits by 3-9x, why blended and incremental CAC diverge by 60x, and how to build a measurement stack that separates real signal from noise.

Python, Next.js, TypeScript, Visx, Recharts

## Get in Touch

I'm currently looking for senior data science roles. If you're hiring or just want to talk shop: [pfelix828@gmail.com](mailto:pfelix828@gmail.com)
